csharp.note: 4 consecutive clean runs; F6 flake gone

low_coverage_files: PLang/app/type/this.cs (line 62.3% / branch 33.3% — Promote throw branch unverified)

findings.issue: The headline F1 change — Promote() throws InvalidOperationException on unstamped non-primitive schema-property reads (commit 3c1521c20) — has zero test coverage. The test named DataType_OnUnstampedData_ThrowsHard_NoSilentFallback asserts ClrType IsNull() instead: no throw, and ClrType never calls Promote() (the throw is on Fields/Values/Shape/Example/etc.). Test name advertises a contract the body does not test.
findings.impact: Deletion-confirmed: replacing the throw with `return this` keeps 3694/3694 green. A future refactor can silently restore the old silent-empty-fold footgun (which surfaces as wrong LLM prompts / schema decisions far from the bug) and no test catches it. Green test names imply the fail-loud contract is verified when it is not.
findings.suggestion: Add a test reading a fold property (e.g. .Fields) on an unstamped non-primitive type.@this and asserting Throws<InvalidOperationException>; rename the existing test to ClrType_OnUnstampedDomainType_ReturnsNull (what it actually pins) or merge it into the throw test.

findings.issue: GetPrimitiveOrMime_ExternalFallbackCallSites_AllRemoved does `if (!File.Exists(path)) continue;` — if the BaseDirectory->repo-root relative walk breaks, both source files are skipped and the test asserts nothing, passing vacuously.
findings.impact: The structural pin (no consumer-site `?? GetPrimitiveOrMime` chains) silently stops protecting if the runner layout changes. Resolves correctly today, so a real pin now, but fragile.
findings.suggestion: Assert File.Exists(path) (fail when the guard file can't be located) instead of `continue`.

findings.issue: Capture.goal sets %captured% to a hardcoded literal identical to the written value, not an echo of the channel payload. The test pins that the accessor Write path REACHES handler code (Capture must run, else %captured% null -> assert fails) but not that the written VALUE flows through. A corrupted/dropped channel value would still pass.
findings.impact: Value-integrity of the channel-accessor write path is unverified. Reachability regressions are caught; payload-corruption regressions are not.
findings.suggestion: Have Capture echo the channel payload variable into %captured% so a wrong/dropped value diverges from the expected string.

findings.issue: No baseline-tests.md recorded for coder v2 (v1 also lacked it). Required by the coder workflow to separate regression from pre-existing failures.
findings.impact: Regression vs pre-existing separation relies on a fully-green suite rather than a recorded baseline; works this time, fragile in general.
findings.suggestion: Coder records baseline-tests.md before starting each version.

summary: Coder v2 honestly fixes 6 of 7 v1 findings — F2 golden is a real SHA byte-diff, F3 paths are now distinguishable (path vs int), F4 reads the type entity via !Type, F5 asserts the typed ChannelNotFound key on a literal-channel index-miss, F6 flake gone (4 clean runs), builder .pr files all match step text. C# 3694/3694, PLang 253/253. But the ONE new behavior added this version — the type.Promote() fail-loud throw, the headline of plan F1 — is deletion-confirmed UNCOVERED, and the test named ...ThrowsHard_NoSilentFallback asserts a silent null return on a different property (ClrType, which never calls Promote). Per strict-red, a confirmed false-green is FAIL. The fix is one test plus a rename.
